subject: UX & Research
title: The Google Wave Confusion
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Google Wave Confusion usable.

hook.text: Google Wave launched in 2009 with massive hype. Users got in and had no idea what to do with it.

buildup.text: Wave tried to combine email, chat, wikis, and documents in one interface. It was everything and nothing.

discovery.text: There was no onboarding, no clear use case, and no guide. Power users loved it. Everyone else was lost.

twist.text: Google shut down Wave in 2010, just one year later. Brilliant tech with zero UX clarity.

climax.text: A product needs a clear 'what is this?' answer. Users who can't explain it to friends won't invite them.

punchline.text: If users can't explain what your product does, it's already dead.

quiz.question: Why did Google Wave fail despite its advanced technology?
options: Users couldn't understand what it was for or how to use it
It was too expensive for consumers
Google didn't market it at all
